pages.raw_text:
Jack Simpkins, the man alleged
to be hunted high and low by Pink
erton detectives on account OF
Orchards confession duplicating
him in the murder Of Steunenberg,
was in Spokane all day Saturday.


He left in the evening for a
destination unknown.


Simpkins was recognized by
close acquaintance, although ne
has shaved off his moustache and
his hair is short and he is 20
pounds heavier than when he
dropped out of sight. 1t would be
difficult now to identify him from
published descriptions He was
about town all the afternoon and
was seen frequently in front of
Durkin's on Howard st.


The man whose whereabouts
have been represented as mys-
terry that puzzled the best detec-
tives the Pinkerton's could COM
mand came to Spokane from
Butte. In a conversation he claim
ed that he is making no extraordfn-
airy efforts to avoid detection, but
says he is not anxious to give him
self upon account of the probability
of having to lay in prison for an in
definite period, as befell Haywood
and other members of the miners
federation.


In the course of the miners
trials he has been in and out OF
Spokane several times, has been in
the Occur d'Alenes, in Butte and
Denver and on the coast. In the
meantime he has read accounts of
his presence in Alaska, Mexico and
Arizona and also of the finding of
his decayed corpse in the Idaho
